---
title: "Install the development version"
subtitle: "Download and try the latest *Gribouille* build from `main`, ahead of Typst Universe."
toc: true
engine: markdown
---

The development build tracks the `main` branch and is published here as a downloadable package.
Use it to try unreleased changes before they reach [Typst Universe](https://typst.app/universe/package/gribouille).

::: {.callout-warning}
This is not a stable release.
It is rebuilt from `main` and can change without notice, so pin a stable version for production documents.
:::

## Download

- [`gribouille.tar.gz`](gribouille.tar.gz).
- [`gribouille.zip`](gribouille.zip).

The bundled `typst.toml` carries a comment naming the exact source commit, so you can always tell which build you have.

## Install as a local package

The package version is the UTC build date, `{{< env DEV_VERSION >}}`.
Typst versions are three integers, so there is no `-dev` suffix; the date is the development marker and sorts above any published release.

Extract the archive into Typst's local package directory under that version, then import it like any other package.

On macOS:

```bash
DEST="$HOME/Library/Application Support/typst/packages/local/gribouille/{{< env DEV_VERSION >}}"
mkdir -p "$DEST"
tar -xzf gribouille.tar.gz --strip-components=1 -C "$DEST"
```

On Linux:

```bash
DEST="${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}/typst/packages/local/gribouille/{{< env DEV_VERSION >}}"
mkdir -p "$DEST"
tar -xzf gribouille.tar.gz --strip-components=1 -C "$DEST"
```

Then import it:

```typst
#import "@local/gribouille:{{< env DEV_VERSION >}}": *
```

The `@local` namespace is separate from `@preview`, so this never clashes with a Typst Universe install of *Gribouille*.

## Import without installing

If you would rather not install it, extract the archive anywhere and import the entry point by path:

```typst
#import "/path/to/gribouille-{{< env DEV_VERSION >}}/lib.typ": *
```
